remember: Students will be able to recall the meaning and importance of international trade in the context of India's economy.
Students will be able to identify the major trading partners of India.
Students will be able to list the main items of India's export and import.
understand: Students will be able to explain the concept of balance of trade and its impact on India's economy.
Students will be able to describe the role of trade policies in promoting international trade in India.
Students will be able to discuss the significance of foreign exchange in international trade.
apply: Students will be able to apply the concepts of international trade to real-life scenarios, such as the impact of trade agreements on India's economy.
Students will be able to analyze the effects of changes in global market trends on India's trade.
Students will be able to evaluate the benefits and drawbacks of different trade policies on India's international trade.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the trends and patterns of India's international trade over time.
Students will be able to compare and contrast the trade relationships of India with other countries.
Students will be able to assess the impact of international trade on India's economic development and regional disparities.

ncert_objectives: As per NCERT objective 1, to understand the importance of international trade in the Indian economy.
To identify the major components of India's international trade, including exports, imports, and balance of trade.
To analyze the role of government policies and international trade agreements in promoting India's international trade.
board_exam_objectives: What are the main features of India's international trade?
How does international trade affect India's economy?
What are the major challenges and opportunities faced by India in international trade?
